name: MoveIt Planner
description: Create a MoveItPlanner from a connected RosClient, loading its robot cell from ROS in one step (MoveIt's cell can only come from the URDF/SRDF loaded into move_group). Tools and rigid bodies wired in are registered into the cell and uploaded as collision objects. The default case needs only ros_client.

inputParameters.name: ros_client
inputParameters.description: Connected RosClient (from the ROS Client component).

inputParameters.name: tools
inputParameters.description: Optional list of ToolModels to register into the cell. Each is keyed by its `.name`. Re-applied on every refresh without refetching.

inputParameters.name: rigid_bodies
inputParameters.description: Optional list of RigidBodies to register into the cell. Each is keyed by its `.name` (set it on the Rigid Body From… component).

inputParameters.name: load_geometry
inputParameters.description: If True (default when unwired), fetch mesh geometry. Set to False for faster loads when only kinematics are needed. A Boolean Toggle is auto-created on this input when nothing is wired.

inputParameters.name: options
inputParameters.description: Optional MoveItPlannerOptions (from the MoveIt Planner Options component) carrying advanced load parameters (URDF/SRDF names, HTTP file server URL). Not needed for standard ROS setups.

inputParameters.name: reload
inputParameters.description: Force a refetch from ROS (bypass the sticky cache). The cell is loaded automatically on the first run; flip this to re-fetch after the robot description or geometry changes. Defaults to False.

outputParameters.name: planner
outputParameters.description: MoveItPlanner with the loaded cell uploaded to the planning scene. Wire it into the planning / IK / FK components.

outputParameters.name: robot_cell
outputParameters.description: The loaded RobotCell (with any registered tools / rigid bodies). Handy for visualization or building cell states without a separate Deconstruct Planner component.

outputParameters.name: detected_distro
outputParameters.description: ROS distribution detected by the client (e.g. 'noetic', 'jazzy'). Useful for diagnosing ROS 1 vs ROS 2 loader mismatches.
